title: CNN architectures (AlexNet → ResNet → EfficientNet) + DSA Tries
focus: What problem each generation solved. Residuals, 1×1 convs, depthwise separable.

items.label: Implement Trie Prefix Tree
interviewQuestions: Why a trie over a hash map — what queries does the trie make cheaper?
What's the time/space trade-off vs storing all suffixes?
How would you support deletion or wildcard matching efficiently?

items.label: Design Add And Search Words Data Structure
interviewQuestions: Why a trie over a hash map — what queries does the trie make cheaper?
What's the time/space trade-off vs storing all suffixes?
How would you support deletion or wildcard matching efficiently?

items.label: AlexNet → VGG → Inception → ResNet → DenseNet → EfficientNet
interviewQuestions: What problem did ResNet's residual connections actually solve?
Why did 1×1 convs become so important (Inception, bottleneck blocks)?

items.label: Why ResNet works: identity mappings
interviewQuestions: Explain why training error went UP with depth before ResNet.
Walk me through a residual block.

items.label: Depthwise separable convs (MobileNet, EfficientNet)
interviewQuestions: How do depthwise separable convolutions reduce compute?
What does EfficientNet's compound scaling do that one-axis scaling doesn't?
